decision: Normalized /v0/login and /v0/changepassword to JSON envelopes and updated OpenAPI for those endpoint contracts.
reason: WAVE04 direction calls for stable JSON responses; this keeps full JWT issuance intact while removing ad hoc string/plaintext success responses.
impact: Clients can rely on JSON envelopes for the touched auth endpoints, and backend/docs/openapi.yaml reflects the new success/error payload shapes.

decision: Centralized mustChangePassword enforcement at authenticated user/profile handler call sites while leaving /v0/login and /v0/changepassword exempt.
reason: This satisfies the acceptance criteria with the smallest boundary-safe change and avoids changing the broader login flow or JWT issuance model.
impact: Authenticated profile and user actions now return the auth helper forbidden result when password rotation is required.

decision: Enforce mustChangePassword on authenticated profile and user handlers other than login and change-password
reason: Existing profile/private-user endpoints were still validating tokens without applying the password-change gate, which left a privilege gap contrary to WAVE04 acceptance.
impact: Private profile, profile mutation, and authenticated user-position routes now consistently deny access with 403 until password change completes.

decision: Keep full JWT issuance on /v0/login but normalize login and change-password responses to stable JSON envelopes
reason: The task explicitly keeps the current login model in place while moving the touched auth endpoints toward the WAVE04 contract direction and removing changepassword plaintext special-casing.
impact: Clients can rely on JSON {ok,result} success envelopes and JSON {ok,reason} failures for the touched auth endpoints without a broader auth redesign.
